module.name: Lone Worker
module.changelog: *1.0.8* Session log now records the alarm call log: which responders were called (cascade), which responder answered, and which responder took charge — shown in each session's timeline and as a "taken charge by N" badge. *1.0.7* Each session now gets a stable, guaranteed-unique ID (assigned at arm, stored on the session and stamped on every one of its events); the Session log shows and searches by it. Sessions are grouped by that ID when present (robust), with reconstruction as a fallback for pre-1.0.7 history. *1.0.6* Add a per-session log (Session log view): browse active and past sessions, each expandable to its full timeline (arm, check-ins, reminders, alarm, re-calls, take-charge, disarm) with summary badges, duration, end reason; period filter (today/7d/30d/all) and live extension/name search. *1.0.5* Consistent navigation bar (Dashboard / Active sessions / Event history / Settings) on every view, so you can always return to the Dashboard; fixed the stale "Active sessions" link that pointed to the Dashboard. *1.0.4* Add a live Dashboard (auto-updating, no page refresh): KPI cards, live sessions with countdowns, announcement queue, system health, live event feed, plus a Reports section (today/7d/30d) with charts, per-operator stats, average take-charge time and CSV export. *1.0.3* Move the announcement queue to a DB table: the kvstore was cached per-process, so the CLI and the drain AGI clobbered each other's queue (lost/duplicated announcements). Single drain channel now enforced via live channel state. *1.0.2* Fix back-to-back announcements: a single paging channel now drains the whole queue (no re-paging collision), so two simultaneous alarms are both announced on the speakers. *1.0.1* Ship the Italian announcement audio built into the module (fixed set, not editable); add per-message "Play on speakers" preview buttons. *1.0.0* First release: native multi-operator lone worker monitoring.
module.description: Multi-operator "lone worker" / man-down safety module. Each operator arms a session from their own extension (701), gets reminders on the speakers, confirms presence (702) or disarms (703). If the timeout elapses with no confirmation, an alarm is announced on the speakers and calls are placed simultaneously to the configured responders (internal extensions and external numbers); the first to press 1 takes charge and the other calls stop. Independent sessions per extension, serialised announcements, full event history.
